what_it_does: Defines a React AI Studio canvas for image generation, image editing, multi-reference composition, mask-based inpainting, chat-based image work, preset management, pipeline execution, result adoption, downloads, and local library picking through window-scoped ConceptV/OpenAI/image-store services.
observations: The file is self-contained and assigns AIStudio to window.AIStudio at the end.
Modes are declared as local data and drive conditional rendering for generate, edit, compose, mask, and chat workflows.
Most behavior depends on global window services: cvOpenAI, cvImageStore, cvAIPresets, dsaToast, and shared visual components.
The layout is a three-column resizable shell with preset/pipeline rail, central stage, and results/history rail.
Model capabilities control mode disabling, size choices, custom-size support, streaming, partial images, quality, format, background, moderation, and variant count.
Generated or edited images are saved into the AI image store and may later be adopted into system, project, or hub scopes.
Pipeline execution supports generate, edit-after-last-image, and adopt steps with a small status log.

Several local subcomponents render specific surfaces: connection banner, preset rail, prompt card, source picker, library picker, compose rack, parameter bar, results rail, chat lane, save modal, and decorative diamond SVG.
standouts: Combines multiple image workflows behind one shared control surface.
Uses model capability metadata to shape available UI and request options.
Includes both direct generation controls and higher-level preset/pipeline automation.
Maintains streaming partial-image previews alongside final results.
conventions: Uses aliased React hooks such as useState_as and useEffect_as instead of direct imports.
Keeps subcomponents in the same file rather than splitting the canvas into modules.
Uses window-scoped application services instead of imported dependencies.
Follows ConceptV visual naming conventions through cv-as-* classes, cv-panel-head headers, CvIcon, ViShape, and pill buttons.
Uses local async handlers for service calls and stores UI error strings in component state.
Uses empty catch blocks around image-store persistence attempts in generation/edit paths.
concerns: The file has many responsibilities in one component file: API calls, persistence, pipeline orchestration, modal state, library picking, and all view rendering.
PresetRail renders a remove button inside another button, which creates invalid nested interactive markup.
adoptAllResults calls async adoptResult without awaiting completion or collecting failures.
Several image-store add failures are silently swallowed with empty catch blocks.
Dropzone copy mentions dropping files, but the visible file handlers only wire click/upload selection in this file.
runPipeline does not set the shared busy state, so pipeline activity is tracked separately from the generation run state.
Chat mode sends quality, format, and background even if the selected model capability set may not expose each option.
notes: This file is the local AI image studio surface: workflow selection, request assembly, preset/pipeline controls, generated output handling, and supporting UI all live together.
questions: What exact shapes do cvOpenAI model capabilities, generated image outputs, and pipeline definitions have?
Should hub adoption use a distinct hub identifier rather than target.pid from the project selector?
Should failed image-store saves be surfaced to the user or logged?
Is drag-and-drop intended for SourcePicker and ComposeRack, or is the dropzone copy only visual?
Should chat availability be capability-gated like edit, compose, and mask?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file renders a full GPT Image 2-style AI Studio with generate, edit, compose, mask, and chat modes, shared chrome, presets, pipelines, parameters, and results.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make AI image creation feel like a native ConceptV production tool, combining low-level model parameters with design presets, reusable references, local image libraries, and project adoption paths.
novelty: The file combines image generation, multi-reference composition, alpha-mask editing, chat-thread image responses, preset capture, and step pipelines in one file-local studio canvas.
contribution: Contributes a complete AI image workflow shell with mode switching, capability-aware option construction, preset and pipeline affordances, source/reference selection, partial streaming display, and adoption/download handling for generated assets.
summary_for_embedding: React canvas file implementing a ConceptV AI Studio with generate, edit, compose, mask, chat, presets, pipelines, parameter controls, result previews, history, and image-store adoption.
intention_for_embedding: This was for exposing GPT Image-style creation and editing workflows inside the ConceptV design system as a brand-aware, project-connected studio surface.

symbols.does: Renders and coordinates the full AI Studio canvas.
symbols.description: This component owns the studio state for mode, prompt, model parameters, sources, compose references, masks, chat turns, pipelines, presets, results, partials, errors, and modal visibility. It builds the three-column layout and wires each workflow to cvOpenAI, cvImageStore, and cvAIPresets services.

symbols.does: Applies a saved preset to the active prompt and generation parameters.
symbols.description: It marks the preset active, replaces the prompt body, and conditionally copies model, size, quality, output format, compression, background, moderation, and variant count into local state. Missing preset fields leave the current state unchanged.

symbols.does: Builds the options object sent to image generation and edit calls.
symbols.description: It combines prompt, model, resolved size, variant count, and brand enrichment with capability-dependent fields for quality, output format, compression, background, moderation, streaming, and partial images. Custom size is used only when enabled and supported by the selected model.

symbols.does: Stores a streamed partial image at its result index.
symbols.description: It updates the partials array immutably, replacing the entry at the supplied index with the incoming image source. ResultsRail then uses those entries while a generation is busy.

symbols.does: Fetches a URL and returns its Blob body.
symbols.description: This helper converts data URLs or remote image URLs into Blob objects for edit, compose, mask, and pipeline requests that require image inputs.

symbols.does: Runs text-to-image generation for the current prompt and options.
symbols.description: It clears prior errors/results/partials, builds request options, attaches a streaming partial callback when enabled, calls cvOpenAI.generateImage, stores returned results, and attempts to persist each output into the AI image store.

symbols.does: Runs an image edit using the selected source image.
symbols.description: It requires editSourceUrl, converts that source to a Blob, adds it to built options as images, optionally streams partials, calls cvOpenAI.editImage, stores results, and attempts to add outputs to the AI image store.

symbols.does: Runs a multi-reference image composition edit.
symbols.description: It requires at least one reference and enforces the selected model reference-image limit. It converts compose images to blobs, appends image roles and names into the prompt text, calls cvOpenAI.editImage, then records outputs and persistence metadata using the composed prompt.

symbols.does: Runs a mask-based image edit.
symbols.description: It requires both a source image and a mask blob, converts the source to a Blob, sends source and mask through cvOpenAI.editImage, and stores the returned outputs. It shares the same option builder and optional streaming path as other image workflows.

symbols.does: Sends one chat instruction to the Responses image workflow.
symbols.description: It appends the user turn, clears the input, sends size and visual options plus previous_response_id to cvOpenAI.responsesImage, stores the returned response id, appends a Vi turn with revised prompt and images, and persists returned images to the AI image store.

symbols.does: Executes a saved AI pipeline step by step.
symbols.description: It tracks the running pipeline id and per-step log, supports generate steps from presets or prompts, edit steps using the previous image, and adopt steps from AI history into a configured scope. Completion and failures are surfaced through toast, error state, and step status updates.

symbols.does: Saves the current prompt and parameter configuration as a preset.
symbols.description: It validates that a preset name exists, writes the current prompt, model, size, quality, format, compression, background, moderation, and variant count through cvAIPresets.addPreset, then closes the modal, clears the name, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Copies a generated result into the selected destination scope.
symbols.description: It builds AI metadata from the current prompt and writes the image into system, project, or hub storage using cvImageStore.addFromSrc. It accepts an optional scope override and shows a destination-specific toast after adoption.

symbols.does: Starts adoption for every current result.
symbols.description: It loops through the current results array and calls adoptResult for each item. The function does not await those async calls in this file.

symbols.does: Triggers a browser download for one generated image.
symbols.description: It creates an anchor element, sets its href to the image source, chooses a timestamped filename using the result format or png, and programmatically clicks the link.

symbols.does: Reads uploaded compose reference files into data URLs.
symbols.description: It FileReader-loads each supplied image file, assigns base to the first newly read file and reference to subsequent newly read files, attaches the original filename, and appends the entries while slicing to the model reference limit.

symbols.does: Removes one compose reference by index.
symbols.description: It filters the composeImages state array to drop the matching index and keep all other reference entries unchanged.

symbols.does: Changes the role of one compose reference.
symbols.description: It maps over composeImages and replaces only the selected item with a copy carrying the new role value.

symbols.does: Renders the preview-mode connection banner.
symbols.description: This component tells the user that AI Studio needs an OpenAI API key for generation, editing, masks, and chat. Its settings button calls onNav with the settings destination when available.

symbols.does: Renders preset controls, pipeline cards, and pipeline step status.
symbols.description: It displays preset count, saved preset buttons, preset removal, save-current action, pipeline count, run buttons, descriptions, and ordered step lists. Pipeline rows reflect running, ok, and fail statuses from pipelineLog.

symbols.does: Renders the shared prompt editor and brand-enrichment toggle.
symbols.description: It shows a prompt textarea with optional placeholder text, a checkbox for brand enrichment, and a remaining-character counter based on model capability maxPromptChars or a 32000 fallback.

symbols.does: Renders upload and library selection for a single source image.
symbols.description: It manages local picker-open state and file input access, reads selected files into data URLs, previews the chosen image, allows clearing it, and opens LibraryPicker for selecting from stored images.

symbols.does: Reads one selected source file and passes its data URL upward.
symbols.description: This SourcePicker-local async helper wraps FileReader in a promise, reads the file as a data URL, and calls onChoose with the resulting source string.

symbols.does: Renders a scoped image-library picker.
symbols.description: It lets the user switch between system, project, and AI history scopes, optionally select a project, and choose an image tile from cvImageStore lists. The chosen image source is passed to the parent picker.

symbols.does: Renders the multi-reference image rack for compose mode.
symbols.description: It provides a multiple-file upload input, empty-state dropzone text, a grid of reference thumbnails, index badges, removal buttons, and role buttons for base, reference, style, object, and identity.

symbols.does: Renders generation parameters and advanced controls.
symbols.description: It exposes model, size, custom size, variant count, quality, adoption target, preset saving, and an expandable advanced section for format, compression, background, moderation, streaming, and partial frame count. Available controls are shaped by the supplied capability object.

symbols.does: Renders generation results, streaming placeholders, adoption actions, downloads, edit-again links, and recent history.
symbols.description: It switches between busy skeletons with partial images, an empty results state, and final result cards. It also lists recent AI history items that can be sent back into edit mode.

symbols.does: Renders the chat-based image workflow.
symbols.description: It keeps the feed scrolled to the newest turn, displays empty state, user and Vi message bubbles, revised-prompt details, returned images, busy status, reset control, and a textarea composer that sends on Enter without Shift.

symbols.does: Renders the modal for naming and saving the current preset.
symbols.description: It displays explanatory copy, an autofocus name input, cancel and save actions, and closes when the backdrop is clicked. Save is disabled until the name has non-whitespace content.

symbols.does: Renders a decorative diamond SVG used in empty states.
symbols.description: It draws a stroked diamond with dashed diagonal cross-lines using the accent bronze CSS variable. ResultsRail and ChatLane use it as an empty-state visual marker.
